* [Anvil](/wiki/Anvil,_Michigan "Anvil, Michigan") * [Connorville](/wiki/Connorville,_Michigan "Connorville, Michigan") * [Dunham](/wiki/Dunham,_Michigan "Dunham, Michigan") * [Hartley](/wiki/Hartley,_Michigan "Hartley, Michigan") * [Puritan](/wiki/Bessemer_Township,_Michigan "Bessemer Township, Michigan") * [Siemens](/wiki/Siemens,_Michigan "Siemens, Michigan") * [Tamarack](/wiki/Tamarack,_Michigan "Tamarack, Michigan") * [Thayer](/wiki/Thayer,_Michigan "Thayer, Michigan") * [Thomaston](/wiki/Wakefield,_Michigan "Wakefield, Michigan") * [Tula](/wiki/Wakefield_Township,_Michigan "Wakefield Township, Michigan") * [Wellington](/wiki/Wellington,_Michigan "Wellington, Michigan")

is an [unincorporated community](/wiki/Unincorporated_area#United_States "Unincorporated area") and [census-designated place](/wiki/Census-designated_place "Census-designated place") (CDP) in [Watersmeet Township, Michigan](/wiki/Watersmeet_Township,_Michigan "Watersmeet Township, Michigan"). As of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_Census "2020 United States Census"), the CDP had a population of 408, out of a total population in the township of 1,456. The community was [platted](/wiki/Plat "Plat") in 1884, designated as a station of the Milwaukee Lake Shore and Western Railroad.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

**Watersmeet** is an [unincorporated community](/wiki/Unincorporated_area#United_States "Unincorporated area") and [census-designated place](/wiki/Census-designated_place "Census-designated place") (CDP) in [Watersmeet Township, Michigan](/wiki/Watersmeet_Township,_Michigan "Watersmeet Township, Michigan"). As of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_Census "2020 United States Census"), the CDP had a population of 408, out of a total population in the township of 1,456. The community was [platted](/wiki/Plat "Plat") in 1884, designated as a station of the Milwaukee Lake Shore and Western Railroad.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Watersmeet,_Michigan&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

The community is located in the northern part of Watersmeet Township, in the western part of Michigan's [Upper Peninsula](/wiki/Upper_Peninsula "Upper Peninsula"), at the [confluence](/wiki/Confluence "Confluence") of Duck Creek with the Middle Branch of the [Ontonagon River](/wiki/Ontonagon_River "Ontonagon River"). U.S. Routes [2](/wiki/U.S._Route_2 "U.S. Route 2") and [45](/wiki/U.S._Route_45 "U.S. Route 45") cross at the southern edge of town; US 2 leads southeast 30 miles (48 km) to [Iron River](/wiki/Iron_River,_Michigan "Iron River, Michigan") and northwest 41 miles (66 km) to [Wakefield](/wiki/Wakefield,_Michigan "Wakefield, Michigan"), while US 45 leads north 46 miles (74 km) to [Ontonagon](/wiki/Ontonagon,_Michigan "Ontonagon, Michigan") on [Lake Superior](/wiki/Lake_Superior "Lake Superior") and south 26 miles (42 km) to [Eagle River, Wisconsin](/wiki/Eagle_River,_Wisconsin "Eagle River, Wisconsin"). The community is also served by the [State Line Trail](/wiki/State_Line_Trail "State Line Trail").[[6]](#cite_note-Pure_Michigan-6)

The community of Watersmeet was listed as a newly-organized census-designated place for the 2010 census, meaning it now has officially defined boundaries and population statistics for the first time.[[7]](#cite_note-7) According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the Watersmeet CDP has an area of 9.2 square miles (23.8 km2), of which 0.004 square miles (0.01 km2), or 0.05%, is water.[[1]](#cite_note-CenPopGazetteer2020-1)
